task_completion.description: Navigate to the Work Items or Bugs list in the TailwindTraders project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser showing the Azure DevOps interface for the 'TailwindTraders' project, specifically on a page listing work items or bugs.

task_completion.description: Designate the bug with the lowest ID (among the three File Upload/Crash bugs) as Primary and ensure it is 'Active' or 'New'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bug related to 'File Upload' or 'Crash' with the lowest ID is visible, and its State field is clearly set to 'Active' or 'New'.

task_completion.description: Link one of the higher ID bugs as a duplicate of the primary bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Links tab/section of either the primary bug or the first duplicate bug shows a 'Duplicate' relationship connecting the two specific work items.

task_completion.description: Link the other higher ID bug as a duplicate of the primary bug.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Links tab/section of either the primary bug or the second duplicate bug shows a 'Duplicate' relationship connecting the two specific work items.

task_completion.description: Set the state of the two duplicate bugs to 'Resolved' and the reason to 'Duplicate'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The two higher ID bugs are shown with their State dropdown set to 'Resolved' and the Reason field explicitly showing 'Duplicate'.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with Azure DevOps rather than using terminal scripts or API calls.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a standard web browser (like Edge or Chrome) navigating the Azure DevOps web interface.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Azure DevOps application and did not mock the interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI elements, menus, and URLs (http://localhost/DefaultCollection) match a genuine Azure DevOps Server instance, not a text editor or locally fabricated HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to superficially alter the text on the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the browser's 'Inspect Element' or Developer Tools pane being open to fake the bug states or titles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project: TailwindTraders. Environment: Azure DevOps Server 2022 Express on Windows 11. CRITICAL NOTE: The underlying dataset in this environment does NOT actually contain any bugs related to 'File Upload' or 'Crash' (existing bugs are about pricing, API errors, inventory, and dashboards). If the agent successfully completes the task, it must have created these bugs itself to fulfill the prompt. Score strictly based on visual evidence of the Azure DevOps UI showing the required bug states, titles, and duplicate links as requested by the task description.
